title: Sensitive-Office Nationality Restriction Expands into General Civic Disability

violations.source: Article IX Section 4.2, Section 4.5, and Section 5.3
violations.summary: Congress imposes a blanket exclusion of all dual citizens on eligibility for a wide class of senior federal offices without showing that the rule is narrowly tailored to an office involving exceptional national-security responsibility. Article IX permits targeted nationality, loyalty, or disclosure rules for especially sensitive offices, but not overbroad civic hierarchy by implication.

satisfied_duties.duty: invalidate or narrowly construe the overbroad sensitive-office restriction so that it does not create a general hierarchy of citizenship or nationality
satisfied_duties.outcome: held that the restriction was overbroad, limited any valid nationality safeguards to genuinely exceptional national-security offices, and barred use of the rule as a general civic disability
satisfied_duties.source: Article IX Section 4.2, Section 4.5, and Section 5.3

ai_summary.primary_violation: Congress imposes a blanket exclusion of all dual citizens on eligibility for a wide class of senior federal offices without showing that the rule is narrowly tailored to an office involving exceptional national-security responsibility. Article IX permits targeted nationality, loyalty, or disclosure rules for especially sensitive offices, but not overbroad civic hierarchy by implication.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties
